ranked_movies.title: Children of Men
ranked_movies.reason: Extensively praised as a masterpiece of dystopian cinema with incredible one-shot takes, emotional depth, and hauntingly beautiful scenes. Multiple users highlighted its realism, cinematography, and powerful themes, making it the most discussed film across the provided threads.

ranked_movies.title: The Act of Killing
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently recommended across multiple posts as a mind-blowing, shocking, and unsettling documentary that changes one's perspective. It was mentioned in requests for both traumatizing films and world-changing documentaries.

ranked_movies.title: Irreversible
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as a film that will 'severely fuck you up.' Noted for its intentional use of sound to induce physical discomfort and its deeply disturbing, thematic content that goes beyond mere shock value.

ranked_movies.title: Three Identical Strangers
ranked_movies.reason: A top recommendation in the documentary threads, described as shocking and mind-blowing. Its compelling true story was highlighted by multiple users as a must-watch.

ranked_movies.title: Happiness
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a chilling 'comedy' that traumatizes viewers with its dark, intersecting storylines and themes of child neglect and SA. It was a standout suggestion in the request for intentionally disturbing films.

ranked_movies.title: The Jinx
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended in the documentary threads for its shocking and impactful true crime story. It was described as a series that leaves a lasting impression.

ranked_movies.title: Mysterious Skin
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as a film that 'shook me to my core' and is extremely dark. It was a key recommendation for those seeking a genuinely good movie that fucks you up with intention.

ranked_movies.title: Lost in Translation
ranked_movies.reason: Top recommendation for movies about 'people being real.' Praised for its beautifully written human moments, awkwardness, and growth, fitting the request for poetic, non-explosive cinema.

ranked_movies.title: Galaxy Quest
ranked_movies.reason: Unexpectedly praised in multiple comments as a fantastic comedy with a perfect cast. Users mentioned having low expectations but being blown away by its quality and humor.

ranked_movies.title: Watership Down
ranked_movies.reason: Directly recommended as a film that 'will fuck you the fuck up.' Its traumatic impact was highlighted in the search for movies similar to Requiem for a Dream.

ranked_movies.title: Kids
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a traumatizing film with its ending 'burned in memory.' It was part of a list of movies dealing with dark, real-world themes like HIV and teenagers.

ranked_movies.title: Good Will Hunting
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its focus on human moments, grief, love, and growth. It was a popular answer to the request for movies with no spies or aliens, just real people.

ranked_movies.title: The Station Agent
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted as an early Peter Dinklage role and a perfect example of a movie about quiet, human moments and connection.

ranked_movies.title: Logan
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as a movie that exceeded all expectations, described as a brutal, fantastic masterpiece that stands alone in the superhero genre.

ranked_movies.title: The Emperor's New Groove
ranked_movies.reason: Unexpectedly praised as one of Disney's best, despite a troubled production. Users mentioned it earned its love through its originality and charm.

ranked_movies.title: Shaun of the Dead
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned as a film that was far better than expected, with a friend apologizing for initially doubting its quality. It's a standout in comedy-horror.

ranked_movies.title: Pirates of the Caribbean
ranked_movies.reason: Noted as a surprise hit based on a Disney ride that became a massive juggernaut. Its success was completely unexpected.

ranked_movies.title: EuroTrip
ranked_movies.reason: Celebrated in its own thread as an excellent dumb comedy with memorable moments and one of the best movie tie-in songs ('Scotty Doesn't Know').

ranked_movies.title: The Shining
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a deep analysis thread discussing Jack Torrance's character and the film's critique of creative men avoiding responsibility, sparking significant debate.

ranked_movies.title: Hereditary
ranked_movies.reason: Referenced in the 'Weapons' discussion as a prime example of modern 'elevated horror' that uses its genre to explore deeper themes like grief and trauma.
